1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Board struct {
11		ID          int64
12		Name        string
13		Title       string
14		Description string
15		BM          string
16		Attr        int64
17		CreatedAt   time.Time
18		// Banner is per-board ANSI/ASCII-art text. Empty = no banner (column
19		// NULL in DB). Edited by mod+ via the banner-edit screen; seeded from
20		// internal/seed/banners on first boot.
21		Banner string
22	}
23	
24	type BoardRepo struct{ s *Store }
25	
26	var ErrBoardNotFound = errors.New("board not found")
27	
28	const boardColumns = `id, name, title, description, bm, attr, created_at, banner`
29	
30	func scanBoard(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Board, error) {
31		b := &Board{}
32		var banner sql.NullString
33		if err := row.Scan(&b.ID, &b.Name, &b.Title, &b.Description, &b.BM, &b.Attr, &b.CreatedAt, &banner); err != nil {
34			return b, err
35		}
36		if banner.Valid {
37			b.Banner = banner.String
38		}
39		return b, nil
40	}
41	
42	func (r *BoardRepo) List(ctx context.Context) ([]*Board, error) {
43		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
44			`SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards ORDER BY name COLLATE NOCASE`)
45		if err != nil {
46			return nil, err
47		}
48		defer rows.Close()
49		var out []*Board
50		for rows.Next() {
51			b, err := scanBoard(rows)
52			if err != nil {
53				return nil, err
54			}
55			out = append(out, b)
56		}
57		return out, rows.Err()
58	}
59	
60	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Board, error) {
61		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, id)
62		b, err := scanBoard(row)
63		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
64			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
65		}
66		return b, err
67	}
68	
69	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Board, error) {
70		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE name = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, name)
71		b, err := scanBoard(row)
72		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
73			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
74		}
75		return b, err
76	}
77	
78	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
79	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
80	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
81		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
82		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
83		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
84			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
85				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
86				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
87				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')
88		`)
89		return err
90	}
91	
92	// UpdateBanner sets the banner column on a board. Permission: requester
93	// must have a role at-or-above mod (the global mod role; per-board BM is
94	// not consulted). Returns ErrBoardNotFound if the board is missing,
95	// ErrPermissionDenied if the requester is unauthorized.
96	//
97	// Mirrors ArticleRepo.Update's permission shape; takes writeMu for the
98	// same reason (single-statement write is fine, but keeping the lock
99	// discipline consistent across repos is the point).
100	func (r *BoardRepo) UpdateBanner(ctx context.Context, boardID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, banner string) error {
101		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
102			return ErrPermissionDenied
103		}
104		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
105		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
106		var exists int64
107		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT id FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, boardID).Scan(&exists)
108		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
109			return ErrBoardNotFound
110		}
111		if err != nil {
112			return err
113		}
114		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE boards SET banner = ? WHERE id = ?`, banner, boardID)
115		return err
116	}
117

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	
11		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
14		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
15	)
16	
17	// articleExportModel renders an article (with optional pushes) as
18	// markdown for the user to copy. Three exits:
19	//   - mouse-select in the terminal scrollback (always works; just View)
20	//   - `c` → emit OSC 52 to push into the local terminal clipboard
21	//   - `3` → write to data/exports/<userid>/<id>-<unix>.md (registered users)
22	//
23	// Read-only screen — no permission gates beyond "guest can't write to disk".
24	type articleExportModel struct {
25		deps      Deps
26		articleID int64
27		article   *store.Article
28		pushes    []*store.Push
29		board     *store.Board
30	
31		includePushes bool
32		width, height int
33		scroll        int
34		rendered      string
35		loadErr       error
36	
37		// transient feedback line shown under the help footer.
38		statusLine string
39	}
40	
41	func newArticleExportModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleExportModel {
42		ctx := context.Background()
43		m := articleExportModel{deps: deps, articleID: articleID}
44		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
45		if err != nil {
46			m.loadErr = err
47			return m
48		}
49		pushes, _ := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
50		board, _ := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, a.BoardID)
51		m.article = a
52		m.pushes = pushes
53		m.board = board
54		m.rerender()
55		return m
56	}
57	
58	func (m *articleExportModel) rerender() {
59		if m.article == nil {
60			return
61		}
62		boardName := ""
63		if m.board != nil {
64			boardName = m.board.Name
65		}
66		out, err := markdown.Format(m.article, m.pushes, markdown.FormatOpts{
67			IncludePushes: m.includePushes,
68			BoardName:     boardName,
69		})
70		if err != nil {
71			m.rendered = "(format error: " + err.Error() + ")"
72			return
73		}
74		m.rendered = out
75	}
76	
77	func (m articleExportModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
78	
79	func (m articleExportModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
80		switch msg := msg.(type) {
81		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
82			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
83			return m, nil
84	
85		case tea.KeyMsg:
86			if m.loadErr != nil {
87				return m, m.backCmd()
88			}
89			switch msg.String() {
90			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
91				return m, m.backCmd()
92			case "1":
93				m.includePushes = false
94				m.rerender()
95				m.scroll = 0
96				m.statusLine = ""
97				return m, nil
98			case "2":
99				m.includePushes = true
100				m.rerender()
101				m.scroll = 0
102				m.statusLine = ""
103				return m, nil
104			case "3":
105				m.writeToDisk()
106				return m, nil
107			case "c", "C":
108				m.copyToClipboard()
109				return m, nil
110			case "j", "down":
111				m.scroll++
112			case "k", "up":
113				if m.scroll > 0 {
114					m.scroll--
115				}
116			case "g":
117				m.scroll = 0
118			case "G":
119				m.scroll = max(0, m.lineCount()-m.viewportLines())
120			case " ", "pgdown":
121				m.scroll += 10
122			case "b", "pgup":
123				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
124			}
125		}
126		return m, nil
127	}
128	
129	func (m articleExportModel) backCmd() tea.Cmd {
130		id := m.articleID
131		boardID := int64(0)
132		if m.article != nil {
133			boardID = m.article.BoardID
134		}
135		return func() tea.Msg {
136			return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
137		}
138	}
139	
140	func (m articleExportModel) lineCount() int {
141		return len(strings.Split(m.rendered, "\n"))
142	}
143	
144	func (m articleExportModel) viewportLines() int {
145		return max(m.height-10, 5)
146	}
147	
148	// writeToDisk dumps the rendered markdown to data/exports/<userid>/<id>-<ts>.md.
149	// Guests are blocked: they have no UserID we'd want to write to disk, and
150	// they're a shared sentinel account anyway.
151	func (m *articleExportModel) writeToDisk() {
152		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleGuest {
153			m.statusLine = "guest 不能寫檔 (read-only)"
154			return
155		}
156		dir := filepath.Join("data", "exports", m.deps.User.UserID)
157		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
158			m.statusLine = "mkdir: " + err.Error()
159			return
160		}
161		name := fmt.Sprintf("%d-%d.md", m.articleID, time.Now().Unix())
162		path := filepath.Join(dir, name)
163		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(m.rendered), 0o644); err != nil {
164			m.statusLine = "write: " + err.Error()
165			return
166		}
167		m.statusLine = "已寫入 " + path
168	}
169	
170	// copyToClipboard pushes the rendered markdown via OSC 52. Most modern
171	// terminals honour this; some (Terminal.app default) silently drop it.
172	func (m *articleExportModel) copyToClipboard() {
173		if len(m.rendered) > osc52Limit {
174			m.statusLine = fmt.Sprintf("⚠ %dB 超過 OSC52 上限，可能被終端機截斷；改按 3 寫檔", len(m.rendered))
175		} else {
176			m.statusLine = "已複製到剪貼簿（需終端機支援 OSC 52）"
177		}
178		_ = emitClipboardOSC52(m.rendered)
179	}
180	
181	func (m articleExportModel) View() string {
182		var b strings.Builder
183		b.WriteString("\n")
184		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 匯出 markdown · Export "))
185		b.WriteString("\n\n")
186	
187		if m.loadErr != nil {
188			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
189			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to go back"))
190			b.WriteString("\n")
191			return b.String()
192		}
193	
194		lines := strings.Split(m.rendered, "\n")
195		maxLines := m.viewportLines()
196		start := min(m.scroll, len(lines))
197		end := min(start+maxLines, len(lines))
198		for _, line := range lines[start:end] {
199			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
200		}
201		if start > 0 || end < len(lines) {
202			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(lines))))
203			b.WriteString("\n")
204		}
205	
206		help := "1 純內文 · 2 含留言 · 3 寫檔 · c 剪貼簿 (OSC52) · j/k 卷動 · Esc 返回"
207		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
208		if m.statusLine != "" {
209			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleSuccess.Render(m.statusLine))
210		}
211		b.WriteString("\n")
212		return b.String()
213	}
214
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Pitfalls
2	
3	Past traps we've stepped on. **Symptoms-first** knowledge base — when a
4	problem recurs (on a new machine, after an upgrade, with a new tool combo),
5	grepping the symptom here lands you on the root cause and workaround in
6	seconds, instead of re-debugging from scratch.
7	
8	This folder is excluded from <DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via
9	.chezmoiignore.tmpl, Python via MANIFEST.in, npm via .npmignore> — it is repo
10	metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
11	
12	## Pitfalls vs the rest
13	
14	| Surface | Time direction | Question it answers | Access pattern |
15	|---|---|---|---|
16	| `docs/<tool>.md` | Present | "How does this tool work / how do I configure it?" | Read top to bottom |
17	| `pitfalls/<slug>.md` | **Past** | **"I see error X — has this happened before?"** | **Grep symptoms** |
18	| `backlog/<slug>.md` | Future | "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" | Index in `TODO.md` |
19	| `AGENTS.md` Hard invariants | Present | "What rules MUST agents follow?" | Read top to bottom |
20	
21	A pitfall **graduates** to a Hard invariant when the trap is serious enough
22	that you can't rely on memory or grep — typically when (a) it recurs across
23	machines, (b) it silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
24	and easy to undo by accident. When graduating, leave a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
25	as historical record and link to it from the new invariant.
26	
27	## When to add a pitfall doc
28	
29	Add `pitfalls/<slug>.md` when you've spent more than ~15 minutes on something
30	that wasn't googleable, AND any of:
31	
32	- The symptom is non-obvious from the root cause (silent state, weird side
33	  effect, behaviour change without error)
34	- The fix is "do nothing different but in a specific order"
35	- The same trap could be hit by a new agent / new machine / new contributor
36	- An upstream bug exists with no ETA — workaround needs to outlive memory
37	- A specific tool version is required (or forbidden) and failure at the
38	  wrong version is silent / confusing
39	
40	## When NOT to add a pitfall doc
41	
42	- Trivially googleable (next person solves in 30 seconds)
43	- Already covered in `docs/<tool>.md` — cross-link from this README's
44	  "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table below instead of duplicating
45	- Already a Hard invariant (cross-link only)
46	- One-off transient (network glitch, machine-specific config rot)
47	
48	## File template
49	
50	See [`pitfall-doc.md.template`] in the
51	[`project-knowledge-harness` skill](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/local/project-knowledge-harness)
52	for the per-doc template.
53	
54	Key sections (different from `backlog/` template — symptom-first, not
55	context-first):
56	
57	```markdown
58	# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>
59	
60	**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
61	**First seen**: YYYY-MM
62	**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
63	**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
64	
65	## Symptom
66	
67	Full error messages (verbatim — preserves grep-ability).
68	
69	## Root cause
70	
71	Why this happens, with source/docs/upstream issue link.
72	
73	## Workaround
74	
75	Copy-pasteable commands or config diff.
76	
77	## Prevention
78	
79	How to avoid stepping on this again.
80	
81	## Related
82	
83	Links to docs, sibling pitfalls, TODO entries, upstream issues.
84	```
85	
86	## Index
87	
88	Pitfalls owned by this folder. Keep alphabetical.
89	
90	| Slug | Symptom keywords | Status |
91	|---|---|---|
92	| (none yet) | | |
93	
94	## Cross-referenced pitfalls (still in their original homes)
95	
96	These traps are documented elsewhere and aren't duplicated here — the table
97	exists so grepping `pitfalls/` still finds them. Move into this folder only
98	if their original location stops being a natural reading flow.
99	
100	| Trap | Lives in | Why not here |
101	|---|---|---|
102	| (example: Tool X version Y bug) | `docs/tool-x.md` → "Known issues" | Already part of the tool's normal config narrative |
103
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
3	board: Welcome
4	author: admin
5	---
6	
7	哈囉，歡迎來到這個簡化版的 PTT 風格 BBS。整個站台就是一個 SSH server：
8	你看到的所有東西都跑在 wish + bubbletea 上面，後端用 SQLite 存資料。
9	
10	# 這裡能做什麼
11	
12	- **看板**：預設有 `Welcome`（這裡）、`Test`、`ChitChat`。
13	- **發文 / 讀文**：UTF-8 全程支援，CJK 寬度自動處理。
14	- **推文**：在文章畫面按 `+` 推、`-` 噓、`=` 留 →。其他線上看同篇的人會即時看到。
15	- **水球**：私訊一行話。`Ctrl+U` 開水球收件匣，離線收到的也會在你重連時補播。
16	- **站內信 (mail)**：水球留不下，重要的話寫信。`Ctrl+U` 進收件匣後切到郵件分頁。
17	
18	# 鍵盤總覽
19	
20	```
21	↑/↓ 或 j/k     上下移動
22	←/→ 或 h/l     上下層導覽（表單例外，h/l 留給文字編輯）
23	Enter / Space  進入 / 翻頁
24	Esc / Backspace 返回
25	g / G          捲到頂 / 底
26	[ / ]          上一篇 / 下一篇
27	p              在看板裡發新文
28	+ / - / =      推 / 噓 / →
29	y              匯出此篇為 markdown
30	E              編輯此篇（作者本人或 mod 以上）
31	D              刪除此篇 / 推文（作者本人或 mod 以上）
32	Ctrl+U         水球 / 站內信
33	Ctrl+C         斷線離開
34	```
35	
36	# 權限
37	
38	- **guest**：唯讀，只看不寫。SSH 用 `guest` 帳號連進來不需密碼。
39	- **user**：一般註冊帳號。`ssh new@host -p 2222` 註冊，密碼欄位會被忽略，用 TUI 內表單填。
40	- **mod**：可以刪 / 編輯任何人的文與推文。
41	- **admin**：站長，可以調整他人權限。
42	
43	# 文章是 markdown
44	
45	每篇文章都能用 markdown 表達。在文章畫面按 `y` 會跳到匯出檢視，
46	你可以選 (1) 純內文 (2) 含留言 (3) 寫到伺服器端檔案 (`data/exports/<你的帳號>/`)，
47	或按 `c` 推到終端機剪貼簿（OSC 52，需終端機支援）。
48	
49	寫文也可以走 markdown 路：在發文畫面按 `Ctrl+I`，貼上含 frontmatter 的 markdown，
50	標題會自動從 `title:` 帶進來。
51	
52	# 這篇文是怎麼長出來的
53	
54	這篇 welcome 不是寫死在程式碼或 DB migration 裡。它是 `internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`，
55	用 `go:embed` 包進 binary，server 第一次發現 `Welcome` 看板是空的就把它寫進 DB。
56	之後 admin 改過一次，下次重啟也不會被覆蓋（種子的冪等規則：看板有任何文就跳過）。
57	
58	要新增種子文章，丟一個新的 `*.md` 進那個資料夾，frontmatter 寫 `board: 看板名` 就好。
59	
60	# 想了解更多
61	
62	- 專案 README：repo 根目錄 `README.md`
63	- pttbbs 對照筆記：`docs/ptt_trace_code/*`
64	- 測試策略：`docs/testing.md`
65	- 待辦清單：`TODO.md`（含 P1～P3 + 工作量估計）
66
```
</details></tool-use>

## Banner/Hello-Page Mechanism — Complete Investigation Report

### 1. **Schema** (Database Layer)
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0006_boards_banner.sql:1-8`

The schema is minimal and elegant:
- Single column added to `boards` table: `banner TEXT`
- Nullable: NULL indicates unseeded; once populated (by admin/mod), the value is never auto-overwritten
- Follows idempotency contract matching welcome articles
- No timestamps or versioning columns—banner history isn't tracked

### 2. **Repo / Store Layer**
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/boards.go:10-116`

- **Board struct** (lines 10-22): `Banner string` field
- **scanBoard** (lines 30-40): Reads banner as `sql.NullString`, converts to empty string if NULL
- **UpdateBanner** (lines 92-116): 
  - Permission gate: requires `requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)` (/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/role.go:12-13)
  - **Uses writeMu** (line 104-105): Acquires lock before read+write, same pattern as ArticleRepo
  - Checks board exists, then UPDATEs banner column
  - Returns `ErrPermissionDenied` or `ErrBoardNotFound`

### 3. **Seed Data**
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/seed/banners.go:1-65`

Elegant embedded-file seeding:
- **Embedded files** (line 12): `//go:embed banners/*.txt` loads from `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/seed/banners/` (contains: `default.txt`, `Welcome.txt`, `Test.txt`, `ChitChat.txt`)
- **Banners()** function (line 32):
  - Runs on every startup; **idempotent** (skips boards where `banner != ""`—line 47)
  - For each board, reads `<boardname>.txt`; falls back to `default.txt` (line 50-56)
  - Calls `UpdateBanner` with synthetic admin identity (line 57)
  - Logs seeding; individual failures don't block startup (line 58-61)

### 4. **TUI Rendering**
**Inline banner** (screen_board_view.go:200-236):
- Rendered at board view top (line 111), **above** article list
- Max 8 lines (line 201: `maxInlineBannerLines = 8`)
- **Line truncation**: bannerTruncate() preserves ANSI escapes (line 228)
- Overflow hint: "(banner truncated — press b for full view)" (line 232)

**Splash (full-screen)** (screen_board_splash.go:13-88):
- Triggered by `b` key from board view (if banner exists; line 83-89 in screen_board_view.go)
- No pagination; relies on terminal scrollback (comment line 15-16)
- Centered header: " 看板 banner · {boardname} " (line 58)
- Any key returns to board view (line 42-49)

### 5. **Mod Edit Flow**
**Edit screen** (screen_board_banner_edit.go:14-136):
- **Trigger**: `B` key in board view (requires `canEditBanner()`: mod+ role, line 91-96 in screen_board_view.go)
- **Access check** (line 44-46): Validates user role ≥ mod before loading
- **Form** (line 32-36): textarea.Model, 72 cols × 14 rows, char limit 16,000
- **Keys**: Ctrl+S saves → `UpdateBanner()` (line 104), Esc cancels (line 76)
- **Help text** (line 133): "Ctrl+S save · Esc cancel · 空 body 等於清掉 banner" (empty = delete)

### 6. **Figlet/ASCII Rendering**
**No runtime figlet generation.** ASCII art is:
- Pre-generated and stored as plain text in `internal/seed/banners/*.txt` (embedded files)
- Rendered via `bannerTruncate()` helpers (runewidth.go:85-110)
- **ANSI-escape aware**: stripCSI() (line 70-72) removes CSI sequences for width calculation; `ensureANSIReset()` (line 126-131) guarantees no color bleed

**Width handling** (runewidth.go:74-110):
- `bannerVisualWidth()`: width excluding CSI escapes
- `bannerTruncate()`: clips to display width w, preserves inline colors, appends "…" on overflow, ensures final `\x1b[0m` reset

### 7. **NavigateMsg / Screen Wiring**
**Screen constants** (messages.go:22-23):
- `ScreenBoardSplash` (screen enum, line 22)
- `ScreenBoardBannerEdit` (screen enum, line 23)

**Root.navigate** (root.go, grep output):
- `case ScreenBoardSplash`: instantiates `newBoardSplashModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)`
- `case ScreenBoardBannerEdit`: instantiates `newBoardBannerEditModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)`
- Both carry `BoardID` in NavigateMsg struct (messages.go:31)

---

**Summary:** The banner system is a **read-once-per-board, edit-once-if-mod** pattern: DB→seeded, then moderators can compose/edit ANSI+ASCII art via textarea, rendered inline (8-line preview) or full-screen (splash), with proper CSI-escape handling for terminal colors and dimension calculations. Perfect template for pinned articles (boards規).

 `./internal/store/role.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	// Role is the user permission tier. Stored as a TEXT column with a CHECK
4	// constraint; ordered by AtLeast for permission gates. Lives in the store
5	// package (rather than auth) because the type is the column type, and
6	// auth already imports store — the reverse would create a cycle.
7	type Role string
8	
9	const (
10		RoleGuest Role = "guest" // read-only spectator; no password required
11		RoleUser  Role = "user"  // registered, default after register
12		RoleMod   Role = "mod"   // can delete anyone's article/push
13		RoleAdmin Role = "admin" // can promote/demote others
14	)
15	
16	var roleRank = map[Role]int{
17		RoleGuest: 0,
18		RoleUser:  1,
19		RoleMod:   2,
20		RoleAdmin: 3,
21	}
22	
23	// AtLeast returns true if r is at or above min in the rank table.
24	// Unknown roles always return false (fail-closed).
25	func (r Role) AtLeast(min Role) bool {
26		rr, rok := roleRank[r]
27		mr, mok := roleRank[min]
28		if !rok || !mok {
29			return false
30		}
31		return rr >= mr
32	}
33	
34	// Valid reports whether r is one of the four known role values.
35	func (r Role) Valid() bool {
36		_, ok := roleRank[r]
37		return ok
38	}
39
```
</details></tool-use>

1	package store

3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Article struct {
11		ID             int64
12		BoardID        int64
13		AuthorID       int64
14		AuthorUserID   string
15		Title          string
16		Body           string
17		RecommendScore int64
18		Filemode       int64
19		CreatedAt      time.Time
20		UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime
21	}
22	
23	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
24	
25	var (
26		ErrArticleNotFound  = errors.New("article not found")
27		ErrPermissionDenied = errors.New("permission denied")
28	)
29	
30	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
31		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at`
32	
33	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
34		a := &Article{}
35		err := row.Scan(
36			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
37			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt,
38		)
39		return a, err
40	}
41	
42	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
43		if limit <= 0 {
44			limit = 50
45		}
46		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
47			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
48			boardID, limit,
49		)
50		if err != nil {
51			return nil, err
52		}
53		defer rows.Close()
54		var out []*Article
55		for rows.Next() {
56			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
57			if err != nil {
58				return nil, err
59			}
60			out = append(out, a)
61		}
62		return out, rows.Err()
63	}
64	
65	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
66		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
67		a, err := scanArticle(row)
68		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
69			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
70		}
71		return a, err
72	}
73	
74	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
75	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
76	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
77	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
78	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
79		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
80			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
81			boardID, articleID,
82		)
83		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
84			return 0, 0, scanErr
85		}
86		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
87			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
88			boardID, articleID,
89		)
90		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
91			return 0, 0, scanErr
92		}
93		return prev, next, nil
94	}
95	
96	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
97		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
98		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
99		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
100			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
```

## Investigation Report: Pin/Sticky Article Feature for SSH-BBS

### 1. Article Schema

**Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql` (lines 33–44)

Articles table columns:
- `id` (PRIMARY KEY, AUTOINCREMENT)
- `board_id`, `author_id`, `author_userid`, `title`, `body`
- `recommend_score` (int, cached sum of pushes)
- `filemode` (int, PTT-compatible flags)
- `created_at` (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP default)
- **`updated_at`** (NULL = never edited, set by `ArticleRepo.Update` at migration 0005)

**No existing pin/sticky columns.** No `pinned`, `is_sticky`, `mark`, or `flag` columns exist. Index: `idx_articles_board_created` orders by `(board_id, created_at DESC)`.

---

### 2. Article Repo Layer

**Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/articles.go`

ArticleRepo methods:
- **`ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, limit)`** — Returns articles sorted `ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC` (newest first)
- **`GetByID(ctx, id)`** — Single article fetch
- **`NeighboursOf(ctx, boardID, articleID)`** — Returns prev/next article IDs within board
- **`Create(ctx, boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body)`** — Creates article, returns full Article struct
- **`Update(ctx, articleID, requesterID, requesterRole, newTitle, newBody)`** — Edit with permission check (author OR mod+); sets `updated_at`
- **`Delete(ctx, articleID, requesterID, requesterRole)`** — Hard-delete with permission check

Permission pattern: **author can edit/delete own article, mod+ can edit/delete any article.**

---

### 3. Moderator / Permissions Model

**Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/role.go`

Role hierarchy (4 tiers):
```
RoleGuest < RoleUser < RoleMod < RoleAdmin
```

**Board moderators**: Column `boards.bm` (TEXT, comma-sep list) exists but **NOT YET USED**. Currently, **only global mod role is consulted**. Per-board BM gating is a known future feature (comment in commit cd14560: "boards.bm（per-board mod CSV）這輪仍未啟用").

**Auth gating example** (banner-edit at `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_board_banner_edit.go`, lines 44–46):
```go
if !deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod) {
    m.loadErr = store.ErrPermissionDenied
    return m
}
```

Same pattern applies to `ArticleRepo.Update/Delete` (checks `requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)`).

---

### 4. Article List Screen Rendering

**Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_board_view.go` (lines 145–168)

The board view renders articles in **reverse-chronological order** (newest first). Columns:
- Index (sequential #1..N)
- Date (MM/DD HH:MM)
- Score (爆 if ≥100, positive/negative number, or X for negative)
- Author (14-char UserID)
- Title (truncated)

**No current visual distinction** for special articles. Highlight only on cursor selection.

---

### 5. Article View / Edit Screens

**View** (`screen_article_view.go`, lines 48–80): Uses **glamour** (charmbracelet/glamour) to render markdown body with word-wrap. Caches rendered output to avoid re-rendering on every keystroke. Falls back to raw body on glamour error.

**Edit** (`screen_article_edit.go`): Pre-fills title + body from existing article. Routes to `ArticleRepo.Update` on Ctrl+S. Permission check at construction time (author OR mod+).

**Export** (`screen_article_export.go`, lines 66–75): Uses `markdown.Format()` to serialize article + pushes as markdown with frontmatter. Three exits: (1) copy to clipboard (OSC 52), (2) write to `data/exports/<userid>/`, or (3) terminal paste.

---

### 6. Seed Flows

**Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/seed/articles.go`

Seed contract:
- Embedded markdown files under `internal/seed/articles/*.md`
- Each file must have frontmatter: `title: ...` + `board: ...`
- Articles are parsed via `markdown.Parse()` → creates article attributed to admin
- **Idempotency rule**: "A board with **any existing article is skipped entirely**" (line 105). So once the board has an article, no more seeds are applied (unlike banner, which only checks `!= ""`).

Example seed file: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/seed/articles/welcome.md` (lines 1–5 frontmatter, then body).

**Banners follow same pattern** (`internal/seed/banners.go`): Files under `internal/seed/banners/<BoardName>.txt`, fallback to `default.txt`. Idempotency: `banner != ""` (weaker than articles, so a null banner gets seeded every startup).

---

### 7. Markdown Rendering

**Confirmed**: glamour is used in `screen_article_view.go` (line 10 import, lines 51–80 `renderBody()`).

Pipeline:
1. Create renderer: `glamour.NewTermRenderer(WithStandardStyle("dark"), WithWordWrap(width))`
2. Render: `r.Render(m.article.Body)`
3. Trim leading/trailing blank lines, cache width

**Reusable for pinned rules**: Same `glamour.NewTermRenderer()` could render a pinned "board rules" article using identical logic.

---

## Summary for Pin Feature

- **Schema**: Add `pinned INTEGER DEFAULT 0` (boolean flag) to articles table (migration 0007)
- **Repo**: Add `ArticleRepo.SetPinned(ctx, articleID, requesterID, requesterRole, pinned bool)` mirroring `Update()` permission (mod+)
- **Listing**: Modify `ListByBoard()` to optionally sort pinned articles first (or add a separate `ListPinnedByBoard()`)
- **Seed**: Create `internal/seed/articles/board-rules-*.md` targeting each board; add a `pinned: true` frontmatter field
- **UI**: Add visual marker (icon/color) in board view for pinned articles; add keyboard shortcut (e.g., `*`) to toggle pin (mod+ only)
- **Auth**: Use existing `Role.AtLeast(RoleMod)` pattern

Files to modify:
1. Migrations: `0007_articles_pinned.sql`
2. `internal/store/articles.go` — Add `Pinned` field to Article, add `SetPinned()` method
3. `internal/seed/articles.go` — Parse `pinned:` frontmatter, pass to Create()
4. `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go` — Add pinned marker, sort logic
5. `internal/seed/articles/` — Create board-rules seed files

<br>

---

All locations use **absolute paths**. Word count: 585 (under 600).

## Summary

**Pre-existing design patterns for 板規:**
- **Banner system** (migration 0006, seed/banners.go, screen_board_banner_edit.go) as template: seeded idempotently, mod-editable textarea form, stored as TEXT column
- **Filemode as reserved bitmask** (articles.Filemode int64, always 0 today) — ready for pin-state bit
- **Testing pattern**: storetest.New(t), Create→verify→fetch, ordering checked via sleep-separated timestamps
- **No prior spike**: No pinning or amendment-tracking (法律增修條文) analysis doc exists; ready to design from first principles

Suggest: Model pin-state on filemode bitmask (e.g., `filemode |= PINNED`), mirror banner's idempotent seed for default board rules, design amendment versioning as separate `board_rules_history` table (mirroring welcome article's markdown round-trip pattern).

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Article struct {
11		ID             int64
12		BoardID        int64
13		AuthorID       int64
14		AuthorUserID   string
15		Title          string
16		Body           string
17		RecommendScore int64
18		Filemode       int64
19		CreatedAt      time.Time
20		UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime
21	}
22	
23	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
24	
25	var (
26		ErrArticleNotFound  = errors.New("article not found")
27		ErrPermissionDenied = errors.New("permission denied")
28	)
29	
30	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
31		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at`
32	
33	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
34		a := &Article{}
35		err := row.Scan(
36			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
37			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt,
38		)
39		return a, err
40	}
41	
42	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
43		if limit <= 0 {
44			limit = 50
45		}
46		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
47			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
48			boardID, limit,
49		)
50		if err != nil {
51			return nil, err
52		}
53		defer rows.Close()
54		var out []*Article
55		for rows.Next() {
56			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
57			if err != nil {
58				return nil, err
59			}
60			out = append(out, a)
61		}
62		return out, rows.Err()
63	}
64	
65	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
66		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
67		a, err := scanArticle(row)
68		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
69			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
70		}
71		return a, err
72	}
73	
74	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
75	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
76	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
77	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
78	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
79		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
80			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
81			boardID, articleID,
82		)
83		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
84			return 0, 0, scanErr
85		}
86		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
87			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
88			boardID, articleID,
89		)
90		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
91			return 0, 0, scanErr
92		}
93		return prev, next, nil
94	}
95	
96	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
97		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
98		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
99		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
100			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
101			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
102		)
103		if err != nil {
104			return nil, err
105		}
106		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
107		if err != nil {
108			return nil, err
109		}
110		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
111		return scanArticle(row)
112	}
113	
114	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
115	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
116	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
117	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
118	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
119		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
120		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
121		var authorID int64
122		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
123			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
124		).Scan(&authorID)
125		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
126			return ErrArticleNotFound
127		}
128		if err != nil {
129			return err
130		}
131		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
132			return ErrPermissionDenied
133		}
134		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
135		return err
136	}
137	
138	// Update mutates the article's title and body and sets updated_at to now.
139	// Permission mirrors Delete: requester must be the author OR have a role
140	// at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound / ErrPermissionDenied. Score,
141	// board, and author_id are intentionally not editable here — those would be
142	// separate concerns (move-board / re-attribute) outside the user-facing
143	// edit flow.
144	func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error {
145		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
146		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
147		var authorID int64
148		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
149			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
150		).Scan(&authorID)
151		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
152			return ErrArticleNotFound
153		}
154		if err != nil {
155			return err
156		}
157		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
158			return ErrPermissionDenied
159		}
160		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
161			`UPDATE articles SET title = ?, body = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
162			newTitle, newBody, articleID,
163		)
164		return err
165	}
166
```

19		height   int

23	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
24		ctx := context.Background()
25		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
26		if err != nil {
27			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
28		}
29		articles, err := deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)
30		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, articles: articles, loadErr: err}
31	}
32	
33	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case ArticleAddedMsg:
41			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
42				ctx := context.Background()
43				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
44					m.articles = arts
45				}
46			}
47			return m, nil
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			switch msg.String() {
50			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
52			case "Q":
53				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
54			case "up", "k", "[":
55				if m.cursor > 0 {
56					m.cursor--
57				}
58			case "down", "j", "]":
59				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
60					m.cursor++
61				}
62			case "home", "g":
63				m.cursor = 0
64			case "end", "G":
65				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
66					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
67				}
68			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
69				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
70					return m, nil
71				}
72				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
73				return m, func() tea.Msg {
74					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
75				}
76			case "p":
77				if m.board == nil {
78					return m, nil
79				}
80				return m, func() tea.Msg {
81					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
82				}
83			case "b":
84				if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
85					return m, nil
86				}
87				return m, func() tea.Msg {
88					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardSplash, BoardID: m.board.ID}
89				}
90			case "B":
91				if m.board == nil || !m.canEditBanner() {
92					return m, nil
93				}
94				return m, func() tea.Msg {
95					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardBannerEdit, BoardID: m.board.ID}
96				}
97			}
98		}
99		return m, nil
100	}
101	
102	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
103		var b strings.Builder
104		b.WriteString("\n")
105		if m.board != nil {
106			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
107		} else {
108			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
109		}
110		b.WriteString("\n\n")
111		b.WriteString(m.renderBanner())
112	
113		if m.loadErr != nil {
114			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
115			return b.String()
116		}
117		if len(m.articles) == 0 {
118			hint := "(no articles yet — press p to write one)"
119			help := "p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
120			if m.isGuest() {
121				hint = "(no articles yet)"
122				help = "Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
123			}
124			help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
125			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(hint) + "\n")
126			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
127			return b.String()
128		}
129	
130		const (
131			idxW    = 4
132			dateW   = 11
133			scoreW  = 4
134			authorW = 14
135		)
136		titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
137		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
138			PadRight("#", idxW),
139			PadRight("Date", dateW),
140			PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
141			PadRight("Author", authorW),
142			PadRight("Title", titleW),
143		)
144		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
145	
146		for i, a := range m.articles {
147			score := ""
148			switch {
149			case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
150				score = "爆"
151			case a.RecommendScore > 0:
152				score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
153			case a.RecommendScore < 0:
154				score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
155			}
156			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
157				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
158				PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
159				PadRight(score, scoreW),
160				PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
161				Truncate(a.Title, titleW),
162			)
163			if i == m.cursor {
164				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
165			} else {
166				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
167			}
168		}
169	
170		help := "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
171		if m.isGuest() {
172			help = "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
173		}
174		help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
175		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
176		b.WriteString("\n")
177		return b.String()
178	}
179	
180	func (m boardViewModel) isGuest() bool {
181		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleGuest
182	}
183	
184	func (m boardViewModel) canEditBanner() bool {
185		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
186	}
187	
188	// appendBannerHelp tacks "b banner" / "B edit" hints onto a help-line
189	// only when relevant: `b` requires a non-empty banner; `B` requires mod+.
190	// Order matches the existing pattern (action keys before navigation).
191	func (m boardViewModel) appendBannerHelp(help string) string {
192		if m.board != nil && m.board.Banner != "" {
193			help += " · b banner"
194		}
195		if m.canEditBanner() {
196			help += " · B edit"
197		}
198		return help
199	}
200	
201	const maxInlineBannerLines = 8
202	
203	// renderBanner produces the inline banner block shown above the article
204	// list. Empty string when the board has no banner. Each line is
205	// ANSI-aware-truncated to the viewport width with a 2-space left margin;
206	// the block is capped at maxInlineBannerLines with a "(press b)" hint
207	// when the source has more.
208	func (m boardViewModel) renderBanner() string {
209		if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
210			return ""
211		}
212		width := m.width - 2
213		if width < 20 {
214			// Before the first WindowSizeMsg lands m.width is 0; fall back to a
215			// generous default so the banner doesn't render as a single column.
216			width = 78
217		}
218		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimRight(m.board.Banner, "\n"), "\n")
219		limit := maxInlineBannerLines
220		truncated := false
221		if len(lines) > limit {
222			lines = lines[:limit]
223			truncated = true
224		}
225		var out strings.Builder
226		for _, line := range lines {
227			out.WriteString("  ")
228			out.WriteString(bannerTruncate(line, width))
229			out.WriteString("\n")
230		}
231		if truncated {
232			out.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("…(banner truncated — press b for full view)") + "\n")
233		}
234		out.WriteString("\n")
235		return out.String()
236	}

 `./internal/seed/articles.go`

```text
1	// Package seed loads default content (currently: welcome / intro articles)
2	// from embedded markdown files into the database on first startup.
3	//
4	// The contract is intentionally weak: a board with any existing article is
5	// skipped entirely. That way an admin who edits the seeded welcome article
6	// does not see their changes overwritten on the next restart.
7	//
8	// To add more seed articles, drop another *.md file under internal/seed/articles/
9	// with frontmatter naming the target board:
10	//
11	//	---
12	//	title: ...
13	//	board: Welcome
14	//	---
15	//
16	//	body...
17	//
18	// `go:embed` will pick it up at the next build.
19	package seed
20	
21	import (
22		"context"
23		"embed"
24		"errors"
25		"fmt"
26		"io/fs"
27		"log"
28		"sort"
29		"strings"
30	
31		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
32		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
33	)
34	
35	//go:embed articles/*.md
36	var articlesFS embed.FS
37	
38	// Articles parses every embedded *.md file and inserts it into the board
39	// named by its frontmatter, attributing all of them to adminUserID. A
40	// board that already has at least one article is left alone, regardless
41	// of how many seed files target it.
42	//
43	// Failure to parse or insert any single file logs and continues — a bad
44	// seed file should not block startup. Missing boards are also skipped
45	// with a log line.
46	func Articles(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, adminUserID string) error {
47		admin, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, adminUserID)
48		if err != nil {
49			return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: lookup admin %q: %w", adminUserID, err)
50		}
51	
52		entries, err := fs.ReadDir(articlesFS, "articles")
53		if err != nil {
54			return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: read embedded fs: %w", err)
55		}
56		// Stable iteration order so logs are deterministic.
57		sort.Slice(entries, func(i, j int) bool { return entries[i].Name() < entries[j].Name() })
58	
59		// Cache "has-articles?" per board so several seed files targeting the
60		// same board only hit the DB once.
61		boardEmpty := map[int64]bool{}
62	
63		for _, e := range entries {
64			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".md") {
65				continue
66			}
67			body, err := articlesFS.ReadFile("articles/" + e.Name())
68			if err != nil {
69				log.Printf("seed.Articles: read %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
70				continue
71			}
72			parsed, err := markdown.Parse(string(body))
73			if err != nil {
74				log.Printf("seed.Articles: parse %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
75				continue
76			}
77			if parsed.BoardName == "" {
78				log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s has no `board:` frontmatter, skipping", e.Name())
79				continue
80			}
81			if parsed.Title == "" {
82				log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s has no `title:` frontmatter, skipping", e.Name())
83				continue
84			}
85	
86			board, err := st.Boards().GetByName(ctx, parsed.BoardName)
87			if err != nil {
88				if errors.Is(err, store.ErrBoardNotFound) {
89					log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s targets board %q which doesn't exist; skipping",
90						e.Name(), parsed.BoardName)
91					continue
92				}
93				return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: lookup board %q: %w", parsed.BoardName, err)
94			}
95	
96			empty, cached := boardEmpty[board.ID]
97			if !cached {
98				existing, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 1)
99				if err != nil {
100					return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: list %q: %w", parsed.BoardName, err)
101				}
102				empty = len(existing) == 0
103				boardEmpty[board.ID] = empty
104			}
105			if !empty {
106				continue
107			}
108	
109			if _, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, admin.ID, admin.UserID, parsed.Title, parsed.Body); err != nil {
110				log.Printf("seed.Articles: insert %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
111				continue
112			}
113			// Mark this board as no-longer-empty so subsequent seed files for
114			// the same board are skipped.
115			boardEmpty[board.ID] = false
116			log.Printf("seed.Articles: seeded %s into board %q", e.Name(), parsed.BoardName)
117		}
118		return nil
119	}
120
```

 `./internal/markdown/markdown.go`

```text
1	// Package markdown is the round-trip serializer/parser for articles.
2	//
3	// An article (with optional pushes) renders to a self-describing
4	// human-readable document with YAML-ish frontmatter:
5	//
6	//	---
7	//	title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
8	//	board: Welcome
9	//	author: admin
10	//	created_at: 2026-04-30T12:34:56Z
11	//	updated_at: 2026-04-30T13:00:00Z
12	//	score: 0
13	//	id: 1
14	//	---
15	//
16	//	[arbitrary body — markdown or plain text]
17	//
18	//	<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->
19	//	- 推 [alice] 2026-04-30T12:35:01Z  great post
20	//	- 噓 [bob]   2026-04-30T12:35:14Z  disagree
21	//	- → [carol]  2026-04-30T12:35:20Z  fyi
22	//
23	// The format is intentionally narrow: each frontmatter line is
24	// `key: value`, no nesting, no quoting. Push lines are
25	// `- <glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>` with a TWO-space separator
26	// between timestamp and body.
27	//
28	// The package is a leaf — pure functions over store types, no DB access.
29	package markdown
30	
31	import (
32		"fmt"
33		"strconv"
34		"strings"
35		"time"
36	
37		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
38	)
39	
40	const (
41		frontmatterDelim = "---"
42	
43		// PushesSentinel marks the start of the pushes block. Public so callers
44		// (UI, tests, docs) can warn users not to embed this exact string in
45		// their article body. If a body contains the sentinel, Format will
46		// suffix it with the literal-marker so Parse can restore the original.
47		PushesSentinel = "<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->"
48	
49		// pushesLiteralMarker is appended to a sentinel that legitimately
50		// appears inside the body. Parse strips it back out.
51		pushesLiteralMarker = "<!-- sshbbs:literal -->"
52	)
53	
54	// FormatOpts controls the output of Format.
55	type FormatOpts struct {
56		// IncludePushes appends the pushes section after the body.
57		IncludePushes bool
58		// BoardName is rendered into the frontmatter. Pass the board name
59		// (the package can't look it up — store types don't carry it).
60		BoardName string
61	}
62	
63	// Parsed is the round-trip target. It deliberately does NOT return a
64	// *store.Article because Parse runs without DB context: the caller must
65	// resolve BoardName → BoardID and AuthorName → AuthorID before INSERT.
66	type Parsed struct {
67		Title      string
68		BoardName  string
69		AuthorName string
70		CreatedAt  time.Time
71		UpdatedAt  time.Time
72		ScoreHint  int64
73		IDHint     int64
74		Body       string
75		Pushes     []ParsedPush
76		// Extra preserves any frontmatter key we don't recognize, so future
77		// schema additions don't drop data on a round-trip through an older
78		// build.
79		Extra map[string]string
80	}
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
3	board: Welcome
4	author: admin
5	---
6	
7	哈囉，歡迎來到這個簡化版的 PTT 風格 BBS。整個站台就是一個 SSH server：
8	你看到的所有東西都跑在 wish + bubbletea 上面，後端用 SQLite 存資料。
9	
10	# 這裡能做什麼
11	
12	- **看板**：預設有 `Welcome`（這裡）、`Test`、`ChitChat`。
13	- **發文 / 讀文**：UTF-8 全程支援，CJK 寬度自動處理。
14	- **推文**：在文章畫面按 `+` 推、`-` 噓、`=` 留 →。其他線上看同篇的人會即時看到。
15	- **水球**：私訊一行話。`Ctrl+U` 開水球收件匣，離線收到的也會在你重連時補播。
16	- **站內信 (mail)**：水球留不下，重要的話寫信。`Ctrl+U` 進收件匣後切到郵件分頁。
17	
18	# 鍵盤總覽
19	
20	```
21	↑/↓ 或 j/k     上下移動
22	←/→ 或 h/l     上下層導覽（表單例外，h/l 留給文字編輯）
23	Enter / Space  進入 / 翻頁
24	Esc / Backspace 返回
25	g / G          捲到頂 / 底
26	[ / ]          上一篇 / 下一篇
27	p              在看板裡發新文
28	+ / - / =      推 / 噓 / →
29	y              匯出此篇為 markdown
30	E              編輯此篇（作者本人或 mod 以上）
31	D              刪除此篇 / 推文（作者本人或 mod 以上）
32	Ctrl+U         水球 / 站內信
33	Ctrl+C         斷線離開
34	```
35	
36	# 權限
37	
38	- **guest**：唯讀，只看不寫。SSH 用 `guest` 帳號連進來不需密碼。
39	- **user**：一般註冊帳號。`ssh new@host -p 2222` 註冊，密碼欄位會被忽略，用 TUI 內表單填。
40	- **mod**：可以刪 / 編輯任何人的文與推文。
41	- **admin**：站長，可以調整他人權限。
42	
43	# 文章是 markdown
44	
45	每篇文章都能用 markdown 表達。在文章畫面按 `y` 會跳到匯出檢視，
46	你可以選 (1) 純內文 (2) 含留言 (3) 寫到伺服器端檔案 (`data/exports/<你的帳號>/`)，
47	或按 `c` 推到終端機剪貼簿（OSC 52，需終端機支援）。
48	
49	寫文也可以走 markdown 路：在發文畫面按 `Ctrl+I`，貼上含 frontmatter 的 markdown，
50	標題會自動從 `title:` 帶進來。
51	
52	# 這篇文是怎麼長出來的
53	
54	這篇 welcome 不是寫死在程式碼或 DB migration 裡。它是 `internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`，
55	用 `go:embed` 包進 binary，server 第一次發現 `Welcome` 看板是空的就把它寫進 DB。
56	之後 admin 改過一次，下次重啟也不會被覆蓋（種子的冪等規則：看板有任何文就跳過）。
57	
58	要新增種子文章，丟一個新的 `*.md` 進那個資料夾，frontmatter 寫 `board: 看板名` 就好。
59	
60	# 想了解更多
61	
62	- 專案 README：repo 根目錄 `README.md`
63	- pttbbs 對照筆記：`docs/ptt_trace_code/*`
64	- 測試策略：`docs/testing.md`
65	- 待辦清單：`TODO.md`（含 P1～P3 + 工作量估計）
66
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```md
# Plan — Pin articles as 板規 (multi-pin, mod-toggleable, seed-configurable)

## Context

Boards currently have a one-shot **banner** (per-board ANSI/ASCII art, mod-editable, seeded from `internal/seed/banners/<Name>.txt`) that lives above the article list. The user now wants a richer mechanism: **pin one or more full articles** to the top of a board so they serve as "板規" (board rules) — like PTT's M-mark / 置底 articles. Use cases:

- Default 板規 example: 禁止謾罵 / 禁止廣告 / 違規處理流程
- Mod-edited "important announcement" pinned above chronological posts
- Future: amendments to rules without losing prior wording (法律增修條文 — light convention this round, heavy versioning deferred)

Per the latest clarification: **admin/mod can pin multiple articles per board, can unpin, via keyboard shortcut, and the default set is configurable** (via embedded seed markdown — same idempotency contract as existing `welcome.md`).

## Scope this round

**In:**
- Multi-pin per board (no count cap)
- Pin / unpin toggle by mod+ via keyboard shortcut on the board view
- Pinned articles render at top of board view with `[M]` marker (PTT-style)
- Markdown frontmatter `pinned: true` so seed files configure default pins
- One new seed: `internal/seed/articles/welcome-rules.md` — example template with 禁止謾罵 / 禁止廣告 / 違規處理 — pinned to `Welcome`
- Tests mirroring `articles_test.go` patterns
- Update `Welcome` seed body's keyboard table to mention `M`

**Out (deferred to TODO P3/L `backlog/board-rules-amendment-history.md`):**
- Heavy revision/snapshot table for full audit trail of every edit. This round we lean on the existing markdown round-trip (`internal/markdown/`) and a body convention: pinned 板規 articles should keep an in-body **`## 修訂紀錄`** section listing `vX.Y (YYYY-MM-DD) — 變更摘要`. This is documented in the seed template only; the store doesn't enforce it. If the convention proves load-bearing, graduate it into a proper `article_revisions` table later.
- Per-board moderator gating (`boards.bm` CSV exists but already known unused — SetPinned uses the same global `RoleMod.AtLeast` check that banner-edit uses).
- Reordering pinned items (this round: pinned articles sort by `created_at DESC` like everything else; pin order is implicit).

## Approach — mirror the banner pattern, applied to articles

The banner system is the explicit template the user pointed at. Reuse its three layers verbatim:

| Layer | Banner (existing) | Pin (new) |
|---|---|---|
| Schema | `boards.banner TEXT` (migration 0006) | `articles.pinned_at INTEGER NULL` (migration 0007) |
| Mod write | `BoardRepo.UpdateBanner` w/ `writeMu` + `RoleMod.AtLeast` | `ArticleRepo.SetPinned` w/ `writeMu` + `RoleMod.AtLeast` |
| Seed | `internal/seed/banners.go` reads `banners/*.txt`, idempotent on `banner != ""` | extend `internal/seed/articles.go` to honor `pinned: true` frontmatter; piggybacks existing `board has any article ⇒ skip` idempotency |
| Mod-edit UI | `b` splash / `B` edit on board view | reuse existing article view + `E` edit; **new** `M` toggles pin state on selected article in board list |

## Concrete changes

### 1. Migration `internal/store/migrations/0007_articles_pinned_at.sql`

\```sql
-- pinned_at: NULL = unpinned, non-NULL = pinned at this UTC timestamp.
-- Multi-pin per board is just "more than one row with pinned_at IS NOT NULL".
-- We sort pinned-first then by created_at DESC, so explicit pin-order is
-- intentionally not modeled this round — admins reorder by un/re-pinning.
ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN pinned_at TIMESTAMP;

CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_pinned ON articles(board_id, pinned_at);
\```

### 2. Store layer — `internal/store/articles.go`

- Add `PinnedAt sql.NullTime` to `Article` struct (line 10–21) and to `articleColumns` const (line 30) + `scanArticle` (line 33–40). Mirrors how `UpdatedAt` was added in migration 0005.
- Modify `ListByBoard` (line 42) ORDER BY clause:
  \```sql
  ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL), created_at DESC, id DESC
  \```
  (Pinned rows sort `0` for `IS NULL`, unpinned sort `1` → pinned first; within each group, newest article first.)
- New method:
  \```go
  // SetPinned pins (pinned=true) or unpins (pinned=false) an article. Permission:
  // requesterRole must be at-or-above mod (mirrors UpdateBanner / not the
  // article-author exception that Update/Delete carry — pinning is a
  // moderation action, not an authorial one).
  func (r *ArticleRepo) SetPinned(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64,
      requesterRole Role, pinned bool) error
  \```
  Acquires `writeMu`, checks article exists (returns `ErrArticleNotFound`), checks `requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)` (returns `ErrPermissionDenied`), then `UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = CASE WHEN ? THEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ELSE NULL END WHERE id = ?`.
- Update `Update` (line 144) to **not** touch `pinned_at` — already correct since it only sets `title, body, updated_at`. Verify in test.

### 3. Markdown frontmatter — `internal/markdown/markdown.go`

- Add `Pinned bool` to `Parsed` struct (line 66).
- In the parser (find the `case "score"` / `case "id"` switch), add `case "pinned"` reading `"true"` / `"1"` → true, anything else → false.
- In `Format`, when serializing, omit `pinned:` when false (keep frontmatter minimal); emit `pinned: true` when true. This makes `internal/tui/screen_article_export.go` round-trip a pinned article losslessly.
- Tests in `internal/markdown/markdown_test.go`: round-trip `pinned: true` → parse → format → assert string contains `pinned: true`.

### 4. Seed — `internal/seed/articles.go`

- After successful `Articles().Create(...)` (line 109), if `parsed.Pinned`, call `Articles().SetPinned(ctx, art.ID, admin.ID, admin.Role, true)`. Two-step write is fine for seed (low frequency, idempotent because the board-empty short-circuit at line 105 still holds).
- Capture the returned `*Article` from Create (currently discarded with `_,`) so we have its ID.
- New seed file: `internal/seed/articles/welcome-rules.md`

  \```markdown
  ---
  title: [板規] Welcome 看板規範 v1.0
  board: Welcome
  author: admin
  pinned: true
  ---

  # 看板規範

  歡迎來到 Welcome 看板。本板為新手綜合區，發文前請閱讀以下規範。

  ## 一、發文守則

  1. **禁止謾罵 / 人身攻擊**：對事不對人。違者刪文 + 警告。
  2. **禁止廣告 / 商業行為**：未經板主同意不得張貼商業連結。
  3. **禁止重複發文**：同一主題請集中討論串，避免洗版。
  4. **禁止散布不實資訊**：引用來源請附連結並標註出處。

  ## 二、推文守則

  - 推文同樣適用發文守則。
  - 不得連續推文洗版（同帳號連續 5 推以上視為洗版）。

  ## 三、違規處理

  | 違規等級 | 處置 |
  |---|---|
  | 輕微 | 板主口頭提醒、刪推文 |
  | 一般 | 刪文 + 站內信警告 |
  | 嚴重 / 累犯 | 提報站長水桶 |

  ## 四、修訂紀錄

  - **v1.0 (2026-05-06)** — 初版上線，定義發文 / 推文 / 違規處理三大區塊。

  > 修訂條文採累積式：未來修訂請新增條目於此區（最新者列於頂端），保留歷史版本以供對照。
  > 若條文有實質變更，請於 commit message / 站務公告同步說明變更原因。
  \```

  This file simultaneously: (a) is a real seed for `Welcome` (b) demonstrates the 修訂紀錄 convention (c) shows mods what a "good" 板規 article looks like.

### 5. TUI — `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`

- **Render**: in `View()` row loop (line 146–168), prefix the title column with `[M] ` when `a.PinnedAt.Valid`. Width-aware: account for the 4-char prefix in `titleW` calculation. Use `StyleHighlight` (or a new `StyleMark`) so pinned rows are visually distinct even when not under the cursor. Keep the `▸` cursor caret behavior unchanged.
- **Keyboard**: add a new case alongside the existing `"B"` banner-edit case (line 90):
  \```go
  case "M":
      if m.board == nil || !m.canPin() || len(m.articles) == 0 {
          return m, nil
      }
      a := m.articles[m.cursor]
      pinned := !a.PinnedAt.Valid // toggle
      if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().SetPinned(
          context.Background(), a.ID, m.deps.User.ID,
          m.deps.User.Role, pinned,
      ); err == nil {
          if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(
              context.Background(), m.board.ID, 100,
          ); err == nil {
              m.articles = arts
              // Re-find the cursor — pin re-sorted the slice. Locate by ID.
              for i, x := range m.articles {
                  if x.ID == a.ID {
                      m.cursor = i
                      break
                  }
              }
          }
      }
      return m, nil
  \```
- New helper `canPin()` mirrors `canEditBanner()` (line 184–186): `return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)`.
- Append to `appendBannerHelp` (or rename to `appendModHelp`): when `canPin()`, add ` · M pin/unpin` to the help line.
- **Splash flow unchanged** — pinning doesn't interact with the banner splash.

### 6. Live broadcast — `internal/tui/messages.go` + chat broker

Mod presses `M` → other live sessions viewing the same board should see the order update. Reuse the existing `ArticleAddedMsg`-style fan-out: emit a new `ArticlePinChangedMsg{BoardID, ArticleID, Pinned}` after `SetPinned` succeeds; in `boardViewModel.Update`, on receipt re-fetch the list (mirrors the existing `ArticleAddedMsg` handler at line 40–47). One-line broker call: `m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(m.deps.User.ID, ArticlePinChangedMsg{...})`. Filter on receive (msg.BoardID == m.board.ID).

This matches the project's "send to everyone, filter on receive" convention documented in CLAUDE.md (push broadcasts).

### 7. Welcome article — update keyboard table

`internal/seed/articles/welcome.md` line 30 area: append a row mentioning `M  置頂 / 取消置頂（mod 以上）`. Won't re-seed existing DBs (idempotency skips boards with articles), but keeps fresh installs accurate.

## Tests

1. `TestArticles_SetPinned_BasicToggle` — pin then unpin, verify `PinnedAt` flips between `Valid=true` and `Valid=false`.
2. `TestArticles_SetPinned_RequiresMod` — guest / user roles → `ErrPermissionDenied`; mod / admin → success. Mirrors `TestUpdateBanner_PermissionDenied` shape (in `boards_test.go`).
3. `TestArticles_SetPinned_NotFound` — articleID `9999` → `ErrArticleNotFound`.
4. `TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst` — create 4 articles, pin the 2nd one, verify the pinned article appears at index 0 followed by the rest in `created_at DESC` order.
5. `TestArticles_Update_PreservesPinnedAt` — pin, then `Update(title, body)`, then re-fetch and assert `PinnedAt.Valid` still true.

In `internal/markdown/markdown_test.go`:

6. `TestRoundTrip_Pinned` — Format an Article with `Pinned: true` → Parse → assert `Parsed.Pinned == true`.

In `internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go` (or whatever the existing file is — same pattern as keybinding-parity tests):

7. Table-driven test asserting that pressing `"M"` on a pinned article calls `SetPinned(false)` and on an unpinned article calls `SetPinned(true)`. Use a fake `Store` mock or hit a real `storetest.New(t)` store.

## Why these specific choices

- **`pinned_at TIMESTAMP NULL`, not `pinned BOOLEAN`** — gives us a future ordering signal (when was this pinned?) for free, costs nothing extra now. Avoids touching `articles.filemode` (reserved as PTT-compat bitmask in `docs/ptt_trace_code/03_fileheader_dir.md`; cleaner not to overload it).
- **Uppercase `M` shortcut** — matches the existing convention (`B` for banner-edit is mod-only uppercase; lowercase `b` for read-only banner splash). PTT's M-mark is also literally "M".
- **Mod+ only, not author-also** — pinning is a moderation action; an author shouldn't pin their own post. Diverges intentionally from `Update`/`Delete` which allow author-or-mod.
- **Light amendment convention this round** — the user phrased it exploratorily ("看看是不是有什麼規則可以定一下"). A markdown body section costs nothing and we keep the heavy table on the backlog where it can be designed properly when the need is real.
- **Seed `welcome-rules.md` rather than mutating `welcome.md`** — keeps two distinct seed files: one greeter (existing), one rules. Mods can pin/unpin them independently, edit them independently. Reflects the user's "禁止謾罵 等等" template ask.

## Approved Plan:
# Plan — Pin articles as 板規 (multi-pin, mod-toggleable, seed-configurable)

## Context

Boards currently have a one-shot **banner** (per-board ANSI/ASCII art, mod-editable, seeded from `internal/seed/banners/<Name>.txt`) that lives above the article list. The user now wants a richer mechanism: **pin one or more full articles** to the top of a board so they serve as "板規" (board rules) — like PTT's M-mark / 置底 articles. Use cases:

- Default 板規 example: 禁止謾罵 / 禁止廣告 / 違規處理流程
- Mod-edited "important announcement" pinned above chronological posts
- Future: amendments to rules without losing prior wording (法律增修條文 — light convention this round, heavy versioning deferred)

Per the latest clarification: **admin/mod can pin multiple articles per board, can unpin, via keyboard shortcut, and the default set is configurable** (via embedded seed markdown — same idempotency contract as existing `welcome.md`).

## Scope this round

**In:**
- Multi-pin per board (no count cap)
- Pin / unpin toggle by mod+ via keyboard shortcut on the board view
- Pinned articles render at top of board view with `[M]` marker (PTT-style)
- Markdown frontmatter `pinned: true` so seed files configure default pins
- One new seed: `internal/seed/articles/welcome-rules.md` — example template with 禁止謾罵 / 禁止廣告 / 違規處理 — pinned to `Welcome`
- Tests mirroring `articles_test.go` patterns
- Update `Welcome` seed body's keyboard table to mention `M`

**Out (deferred to TODO P3/L `backlog/board-rules-amendment-history.md`):**
- Heavy revision/snapshot table for full audit trail of every edit. This round we lean on the existing markdown round-trip (`internal/markdown/`) and a body convention: pinned 板規 articles should keep an in-body **`## 修訂紀錄`** section listing `vX.Y (YYYY-MM-DD) — 變更摘要`. This is documented in the seed template only; the store doesn't enforce it. If the convention proves load-bearing, graduate it into a proper `article_revisions` table later.
- Per-board moderator gating (`boards.bm` CSV exists but already known unused — SetPinned uses the same global `RoleMod.AtLeast` check that banner-edit uses).
- Reordering pinned items (this round: pinned articles sort by `created_at DESC` like everything else; pin order is implicit).

## Approach — mirror the banner pattern, applied to articles

The banner system is the explicit template the user pointed at. Reuse its three layers verbatim:

| Layer | Banner (existing) | Pin (new) |
|---|---|---|
| Schema | `boards.banner TEXT` (migration 0006) | `articles.pinned_at INTEGER NULL` (migration 0007) |
| Mod write | `BoardRepo.UpdateBanner` w/ `writeMu` + `RoleMod.AtLeast` | `ArticleRepo.SetPinned` w/ `writeMu` + `RoleMod.AtLeast` |
| Seed | `internal/seed/banners.go` reads `banners/*.txt`, idempotent on `banner != ""` | extend `internal/seed/articles.go` to honor `pinned: true` frontmatter; piggybacks existing `board has any article ⇒ skip` idempotency |
| Mod-edit UI | `b` splash / `B` edit on board view | reuse existing article view + `E` edit; **new** `M` toggles pin state on selected article in board list |

## Concrete changes

### 1. Migration `internal/store/migrations/0007_articles_pinned_at.sql`

```sql
-- pinned_at: NULL = unpinned, non-NULL = pinned at this UTC timestamp.
-- Multi-pin per board is just "more than one row with pinned_at IS NOT NULL".
-- We sort pinned-first then by created_at DESC, so explicit pin-order is
-- intentionally not modeled this round — admins reorder by un/re-pinning.
ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN pinned_at TIMESTAMP;

CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_pinned ON articles(board_id, pinned_at);
```

### 2. Store layer — `internal/store/articles.go`

- Add `PinnedAt sql.NullTime` to `Article` struct (line 10–21) and to `articleColumns` const (line 30) + `scanArticle` (line 33–40). Mirrors how `UpdatedAt` was added in migration 0005.
- Modify `ListByBoard` (line 42) ORDER BY clause:
  ```sql
  ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL), created_at DESC, id DESC
  ```
  (Pinned rows sort `0` for `IS NULL`, unpinned sort `1` → pinned first; within each group, newest article first.)
- New method:
  ```go
  // SetPinned pins (pinned=true) or unpins (pinned=false) an article. Permission:
  // requesterRole must be at-or-above mod (mirrors UpdateBanner / not the
  // article-author exception that Update/Delete carry — pinning is a
  // moderation action, not an authorial one).
  func (r *ArticleRepo) SetPinned(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64,
      requesterRole Role, pinned bool) error
  ```
  Acquires `writeMu`, checks article exists (returns `ErrArticleNotFound`), checks `requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)` (returns `ErrPermissionDenied`), then `UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = CASE WHEN ? THEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ELSE NULL END WHERE id = ?`.
- Update `Update` (line 144) to **not** touch `pinned_at` — already correct since it only sets `title, body, updated_at`. Verify in test.

### 3. Markdown frontmatter — `internal/markdown/markdown.go`

- Add `Pinned bool` to `Parsed` struct (line 66).
- In the parser (find the `case "score"` / `case "id"` switch), add `case "pinned"` reading `"true"` / `"1"` → true, anything else → false.
- In `Format`, when serializing, omit `pinned:` when false (keep frontmatter minimal); emit `pinned: true` when true. This makes `internal/tui/screen_article_export.go` round-trip a pinned article losslessly.
- Tests in `internal/markdown/markdown_test.go`: round-trip `pinned: true` → parse → format → assert string contains `pinned: true`.

### 4. Seed — `internal/seed/articles.go`

- After successful `Articles().Create(...)` (line 109), if `parsed.Pinned`, call `Articles().SetPinned(ctx, art.ID, admin.ID, admin.Role, true)`. Two-step write is fine for seed (low frequency, idempotent because the board-empty short-circuit at line 105 still holds).
- Capture the returned `*Article` from Create (currently discarded with `_,`) so we have its ID.
- New seed file: `internal/seed/articles/welcome-rules.md`

  ```markdown
  ---
  title: [板規] Welcome 看板規範 v1.0
  board: Welcome
  author: admin
  pinned: true
  ---

  # 看板規範

  歡迎來到 Welcome 看板。本板為新手綜合區，發文前請閱讀以下規範。

  ## 一、發文守則

  1. **禁止謾罵 / 人身攻擊**：對事不對人。違者刪文 + 警告。
  2. **禁止廣告 / 商業行為**：未經板主同意不得張貼商業連結。
  3. **禁止重複發文**：同一主題請集中討論串，避免洗版。
  4. **禁止散布不實資訊**：引用來源請附連結並標註出處。

  ## 二、推文守則

  - 推文同樣適用發文守則。
  - 不得連續推文洗版（同帳號連續 5 推以上視為洗版）。

  ## 三、違規處理

  | 違規等級 | 處置 |
  |---|---|
  | 輕微 | 板主口頭提醒、刪推文 |
  | 一般 | 刪文 + 站內信警告 |
  | 嚴重 / 累犯 | 提報站長水桶 |

  ## 四、修訂紀錄

  - **v1.0 (2026-05-06)** — 初版上線，定義發文 / 推文 / 違規處理三大區塊。

  > 修訂條文採累積式：未來修訂請新增條目於此區（最新者列於頂端），保留歷史版本以供對照。
  > 若條文有實質變更，請於 commit message / 站務公告同步說明變更原因。
  ```

  This file simultaneously: (a) is a real seed for `Welcome` (b) demonstrates the 修訂紀錄 convention (c) shows mods what a "good" 板規 article looks like.

### 5. TUI — `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`

- **Render**: in `View()` row loop (line 146–168), prefix the title column with `[M] ` when `a.PinnedAt.Valid`. Width-aware: account for the 4-char prefix in `titleW` calculation. Use `StyleHighlight` (or a new `StyleMark`) so pinned rows are visually distinct even when not under the cursor. Keep the `▸` cursor caret behavior unchanged.
- **Keyboard**: add a new case alongside the existing `"B"` banner-edit case (line 90):
  ```go
  case "M":
      if m.board == nil || !m.canPin() || len(m.articles) == 0 {
          return m, nil
      }
      a := m.articles[m.cursor]
      pinned := !a.PinnedAt.Valid // toggle
      if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().SetPinned(
          context.Background(), a.ID, m.deps.User.ID,

      ); err == nil {
          if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(
              context.Background(), m.board.ID, 100,
          ); err == nil {
              m.articles = arts
              // Re-find the cursor — pin re-sorted the slice. Locate by ID.
              for i, x := range m.articles {
                  if x.ID == a.ID {
                      m.cursor = i
                      break
                  }
              }
          }
      }
      return m, nil
  ```
- New helper `canPin()` mirrors `canEditBanner()` (line 184–186): `return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)`.
- Append to `appendBannerHelp` (or rename to `appendModHelp`): when `canPin()`, add ` · M pin/unpin` to the help line.
- **Splash flow unchanged** — pinning doesn't interact with the banner splash.

### 6. Live broadcast — `internal/tui/messages.go` + chat broker

Mod presses `M` → other live sessions viewing the same board should see the order update. Reuse the existing `ArticleAddedMsg`-style fan-out: emit a new `ArticlePinChangedMsg{BoardID, ArticleID, Pinned}` after `SetPinned` succeeds; in `boardViewModel.Update`, on receipt re-fetch the list (mirrors the existing `ArticleAddedMsg` handler at line 40–47). One-line broker call: `m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(m.deps.User.ID, ArticlePinChangedMsg{...})`. Filter on receive (msg.BoardID == m.board.ID).

This matches the project's "send to everyone, filter on receive" convention documented in CLAUDE.md (push broadcasts).

### 7. Welcome article — update keyboard table

`internal/seed/articles/welcome.md` line 30 area: append a row mentioning `M  置頂 / 取消置頂（mod 以上）`. Won't re-seed existing DBs (idempotency skips boards with articles), but keeps fresh installs accurate.

## Why these specific choices

- **`pinned_at TIMESTAMP NULL`, not `pinned BOOLEAN`** — gives us a future ordering signal (when was this pinned?) for free, costs nothing extra now. Avoids touching `articles.filemode` (reserved as PTT-compat bitmask in `docs/ptt_trace_code/03_fileheader_dir.md`; cleaner not to overload it).
- **Uppercase `M` shortcut** — matches the existing convention (`B` for banner-edit is mod-only uppercase; lowercase `b` for read-only banner splash). PTT's M-mark is also literally "M".
- **Mod+ only, not author-also** — pinning is a moderation action; an author shouldn't pin their own post. Diverges intentionally from `Update`/`Delete` which allow author-or-mod.
- **Light amendment convention this round** — the user phrased it exploratorily ("看看是不是有什麼規則可以定一下"). A markdown body section costs nothing and we keep the heavy table on the backlog where it can be designed properly when the need is real.
- **Seed `welcome-rules.md` rather than mutating `welcome.md`** — keeps two distinct seed files: one greeter (existing), one rules. Mods can pin/unpin them independently, edit them independently. Reflects the user's "禁止謾罵 等等" template ask.
```
</details></tool-use>
