```text
80	}
81	
82	// ParsedPush mirrors store.Push but with author as a name (not user_id) and
83	// without a DB-side ID; the caller decides whether/how to materialize it.
84	type ParsedPush struct {
85		Kind      store.PushKind
86		Author    string
87		CreatedAt time.Time
88		Body      string
89	}
90	
91	// Format renders an article (plus optional pushes) to the canonical text
92	// form. Returns an error only on a nil article; otherwise it always
93	// produces a valid, parseable document.
94	func Format(a *store.Article, pushes []*store.Push, opts FormatOpts) (string, error) {
95		if a == nil {
96			return "", fmt.Errorf("markdown.Format: article is nil")
97		}
98		var b strings.Builder
99		b.WriteString(frontmatterDelim + "\n")
100		writeFrontmatter(&b, "title", sanitizeFM(a.Title))
101		if opts.BoardName != "" {
102			writeFrontmatter(&b, "board", sanitizeFM(opts.BoardName))
103		}
104		if a.AuthorUserID != "" {
105			writeFrontmatter(&b, "author", sanitizeFM(a.AuthorUserID))
106		}
107		if !a.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
108			writeFrontmatter(&b, "created_at", a.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
109		}
110		if a.UpdatedAt.Valid {
111			writeFrontmatter(&b, "updated_at", a.UpdatedAt.Time.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
112		}
113		writeFrontmatter(&b, "score", strconv.FormatInt(a.RecommendScore, 10))
114		if a.ID > 0 {
115			writeFrontmatter(&b, "id", strconv.FormatInt(a.ID, 10))
116		}
117		b.WriteString(frontmatterDelim + "\n\n")
118	
119		body := a.Body
120		// Disambiguate any literal sentinel inside the body so Parse won't
121		// mis-cleave at it. Best-effort: if the user already has the
122		// literal-marker after the sentinel, we leave it alone.
123		if strings.Contains(body, PushesSentinel) {
124			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker, "\x00LITERAL_MARKED\x00")
125			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, PushesSentinel, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker)
126			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, "\x00LITERAL_MARKED\x00", PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker)
127		}
128		b.WriteString(body)
129		if !strings.HasSuffix(body, "\n") {
130			b.WriteString("\n")
131		}
132	
133		if opts.IncludePushes && len(pushes) > 0 {
134			b.WriteString("\n" + PushesSentinel + "\n")
135			for _, p := range pushes {
136				ts := p.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
137				// Push body is single-line by DB convention; flatten just in case.
138				pb := strings.ReplaceAll(p.Body, "\n", " ")
139				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- %s [%s] %s  %s\n", p.Kind.Glyph(), p.UserUserID, ts, pb)
140			}
141		}
142	
143		return b.String(), nil
144	}
145	
146	// Parse reads markdown back into a Parsed. Permissive: missing frontmatter
147	// or pushes section degrade to zero values; the whole input becomes Body
148	// when no frontmatter is present.
149	func Parse(md string) (*Parsed, error) {
150		p := &Parsed{Extra: map[string]string{}}
151		rest := md
152	
153		if strings.HasPrefix(rest, frontmatterDelim+"\n") {
154			rest = rest[len(frontmatterDelim)+1:]
155			closeIdx := strings.Index(rest, "\n"+frontmatterDelim+"\n")
156			closeAtEOF := false
157			if closeIdx < 0 && strings.HasSuffix(rest, "\n"+frontmatterDelim) {
158				closeIdx = len(rest) - len("\n"+frontmatterDelim)
159				closeAtEOF = true
160			}
161			if closeIdx >= 0 {
162				fm := rest[:closeIdx]
163				if closeAtEOF {
164					rest = ""
165				} else {
166					rest = rest[closeIdx+len("\n"+frontmatterDelim+"\n"):]
167				}
168				parseFrontmatter(p, fm)
169			}
170		}
171	
172		rest = strings.TrimLeft(rest, "\n")
173		if strings.Contains(rest, PushesSentinel) {
174			// The sentinel may be followed by literal-marker, which means it
175			// belongs to the body. Walk past every literal-marked occurrence.
176			head := rest
177			offset := 0
178			for {
179				i := strings.Index(head, PushesSentinel)
180				if i < 0 {
181					// Fell through without a real sentinel — entire rest is body.
182					p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(strings.TrimRight(rest, "\n"))
183					return p, nil
184				}
185				afterSentinel := i + len(PushesSentinel)
186				if strings.HasPrefix(head[afterSentinel:], pushesLiteralMarker) {
187					// Skip — it's part of the body. Continue searching.
188					next := afterSentinel + len(pushesLiteralMarker)
189					offset += next
190					head = head[next:]
191					continue
192				}
193				// Real sentinel — split.
194				cut := offset + i
195				body := strings.TrimRight(rest[:cut], "\n")
196				p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(body)
197				parsePushes(p, rest[cut+len(PushesSentinel):])
198				return p, nil
199			}
200		}
201		p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(strings.TrimRight(rest, "\n"))
202		return p, nil
203	}
204	
205	func writeFrontmatter(b *strings.Builder, key, val string) {
206		b.WriteString(key)
207		b.WriteString(": ")
208		b.WriteString(val)
209		b.WriteString("\n")
210	}
211	
212	func sanitizeFM(s string) string {
213		// Frontmatter values are single-line. CR/LF would break the parser;
214		// flatten to space.
215		s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\r", " ")
216		s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\n", " ")
217		return s
218	}
219	
220	func parseFrontmatter(p *Parsed, fm string) {
221		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(fm, "\n") {
222			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
223			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
224				continue
225			}
226			idx := strings.IndexByte(line, ':')
227			if idx <= 0 {
228				continue
229			}
230			key := strings.TrimSpace(line[:idx])
231			val := strings.TrimSpace(line[idx+1:])
232			switch key {
233			case "title":
234				p.Title = val
235			case "board":
236				p.BoardName = val
237			case "author":
238				p.AuthorName = val
239			case "created_at":
240				if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val); err == nil {
241					p.CreatedAt = t
242				}
243			case "updated_at":
244				if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val); err == nil {
245					p.UpdatedAt = t
246				}
247			case "score":
248				if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64); err == nil {
249					p.ScoreHint = n
250				}
251			case "id":
252				if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64); err == nil {
253					p.IDHint = n
254				}
255			default:
256				p.Extra[key] = val
257			}
258		}
259	}
260	
261	func parsePushes(p *Parsed, block string) {
262		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(block, "\n") {
263			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
264			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
265			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "- ") {
266				continue
267			}
268			if push, ok := parsePushLine(line[2:]); ok {
269				p.Pushes = append(p.Pushes, push)
270			}
271		}
272	}
273	
274	func parsePushLine(s string) (ParsedPush, bool) {
275		// Format: "<glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>"
276		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
277		if s == "" {
278			return ParsedPush{}, false
279		}
```
</details></tool-use>

```sql
-- Pin articles as 板規 (board rules) / important announcements. NULL = unpinned;
-- non-NULL = the UTC timestamp at which a mod+ pinned the article via
-- ArticleRepo.SetPinned. Multi-pin is supported intrinsically: any number of
-- rows on a board can hold a non-NULL pinned_at.
--
-- Ordering: ListByBoard sorts pinned-first then by created_at DESC. We do
-- NOT model an explicit pin-order column this round — admins reorder pins by
-- un/re-pinning (the latest pin gets the newest pinned_at, but display order
-- is still anchored to created_at within the pinned group; if that proves
-- limiting, a follow-up can add `pin_order INTEGER` and a swap operation).
--
-- Idempotency contract mirrors the welcome-article seed: a board with any
-- existing article is skipped entirely (see internal/seed/articles.go), so
-- pinned-by-default seed files only apply to fresh boards.

ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN pinned_at DATETIME;

CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_pinned ON articles(board_id, pinned_at);

```

```text
90	}

92	// UpdateBanner sets the banner column on a board. Permission: requester
93	// must have a role at-or-above mod (the global mod role; per-board BM is
94	// not consulted). Returns ErrBoardNotFound if the board is missing,
95	// ErrPermissionDenied if the requester is unauthorized.
96	//
97	// Mirrors ArticleRepo.Update's permission shape; takes writeMu for the
98	// same reason (single-statement write is fine, but keeping the lock
99	// discipline consistent across repos is the point).
100	func (r *BoardRepo) UpdateBanner(ctx context.Context, boardID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, banner string) error {
101		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
102			return ErrPermissionDenied
103		}
104		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
105		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
106		var exists int64
107		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT id FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, boardID).Scan(&exists)
108		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
109			return ErrBoardNotFound
110		}
111		if err != nil {
112			return err
113		}
114		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE boards SET banner = ? WHERE id = ?`, banner, boardID)
115		return err
116	}
117
```

```md
---
title: [板規] Welcome 看板規範 v1.0
board: Welcome
author: admin
pinned: true
---

# 看板規範

歡迎來到 Welcome 看板。本板為新手綜合區，發文前請先閱讀以下規範。違反者由板主依下列等級處置；對處置有異議可寫站內信向站長申訴。

## 一、發文守則

1. **禁止謾罵 / 人身攻擊**：對事不對人。鎖定特定使用者批評、嘲諷、羞辱者一律刪文。
2. **禁止廣告 / 商業行為**：未經板主同意不得張貼商業連結、團購、推銷。
3. **禁止重複發文**：同主題請集中討論，避免洗版；尋找類似舊文可在標題前加 `Re:`。
4. **禁止散布不實資訊**：引用消息請附來源連結與出處；未經查證的爆料不在此板處理。
5. **發文標題請有意義**：避免「help」、「問一下」這種無資訊的標題；用 `[分類]` 前綴會更好。

## 二、推文守則

- 推文同樣適用上述發文守則。
- 不得連續推文洗版（同帳號連續 5 推以上視為洗版）。
- 推文僅作補充或回應之用，深度討論請另開新文。

## 三、違規處理

| 違規等級 | 處置 |
|---|---|
| 輕微 | 板主口頭提醒 / 刪推文 |
| 一般 | 刪文 + 站內信警告 |
| 嚴重 / 累犯 | 提報站長水桶（暫停發文權限） |

## 四、修訂紀錄

- **v1.0 (2026-05-06)** — 初版上線：定義發文 / 推文 / 違規處理三大區塊。

> 修訂條文採累積式：未來修訂請新增條目於本區（最新者列於頂端），保留歷史版本以供對照。
> 若條文有實質變更，請於 commit message / 站務公告同步說明變更原因。

```

```text
241	// findCursorByID returns the index of the article with the given ID in
242	// the slice, or 0 if not found. Used to re-anchor the cursor after a
243	// pin/unpin reorders the list.
244	func findCursorByID(arts []*store.Article, id int64) int {
245		for i, a := range arts {
246			if a.ID == id {
247				return i
248			}
249		}
250		return 0
251	}

253	// appendBannerHelp tacks "b banner" / "B edit" / "M pin" hints onto a
254	// help-line only when relevant: `b` requires a non-empty banner; `B` and
255	// `M` require mod+. Order matches the existing pattern (action keys
256	// before navigation). Name kept for git-blame stability — it now covers
257	// all mod-affordances on the board view, not just banner ones.
258	func (m boardViewModel) appendBannerHelp(help string) string {
259		if m.board != nil && m.board.Banner != "" {
260			help += " · b banner"
261		}
262		if m.canEditBanner() {
263			help += " · B edit"
264		}

266	}
```

1	# TODO
2	
3	Long-term backlog for sshbbs. See [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md)
4	for the maintenance workflow that agents should follow.
5	
6	> **For agents**: when the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being
7	> implemented this session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have",
8	> "工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add it here with priority + effort tags.
9	> Do not create new `ROADMAP.md` / `IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files —
10	> `TODO.md` is the single backlog index. Long-form research goes in
11	> [`backlog/<slug>.md`](backlog/).
12	
13	<!-- Use the exact section order: P1, P2, P3, P?, Done.
14	     The bundled scripts/todo-kanban.sh validator only inspects top-level
15	     `- [ ]` and `- ✅` items inside these sections. Prose paragraphs,
16	     blockquotes, indented sub-bullets, HTML comments, and `---` rules are
17	     ignored — feel free to add inline guidance like this without breaking
18	     machine readability. -->
19	
20	## P1
21	
22	Likely next batch — items you'd reach for if you sat down to work today.
23	Mostly "PTT-feel polish" carried forward from the original plan's SHOULD HAVE bucket.
24	
25	(P1 batch shipped 2026-04-29 — see Done.)
26	
27	## P2
28	
29	Worth doing, no rush.
30	
31	- [ ] **[S] Soft delete for articles** — add `articles.deleted_at` column + filter in `ArticleRepo.ListByBoard`. Avoid breaking the recno-vs-uuid invariant (see `docs/ptt_trace_code/03_fileheader_dir.md`). When implemented, mod-deletion (now in `ArticleRepo.Delete` from the role work) should populate `deleted_at` instead of `DELETE FROM`, so banned content stays in the audit trail.
32	- [ ] **[S] Audit log of admin actions** — table `audit_log(actor_id, target_id, action, at)` capturing role promotions/demotions and mod deletes. Mod tools without audit are tempting and we already ship them as of the role-permission batch.
33	- [ ] **[M] Per-board moderators** — `boards.bm` is a CSV of userids today (unused). Resolve `bm` against `users.user_id` at the article/push delete check site so a regular user with their userid in `bm` can mod that one board. Currently mod is global.
34	- [ ] **[S] Banned / suspended flags** — orthogonal `users.flags INTEGER` bitmap. `RoleGuest` covers spectator; banning a registered user still needs a flag separate from `role` (e.g. set `flags |= FLAG_BANNED` and refuse SSH at the password callback before VerifyLogin returns).
35	- [ ] **[S] Per-board BM list in header** — `boards.bm` is already populated; just render in `screen_board_view`'s header line.
36	- [ ] **[S] Seed-data CLI** — `sshbbs seed --articles 50` for demo content / load testing. Lift the SQL from `scripts/record-demo.sh` into a proper subcommand.
37	- [ ] **[S] ANSI color art splash screen** — render a small lipgloss-styled banner on the welcome/main-menu screens.
38	- [ ] **[M] Pager settings** — friends-only / do-not-disturb for water balloons. Bitfield on `users` (currently no attr column) + check in `Broker.Send` before delivering.
39	- [ ] **[M] Per-board attr / hidden boards** — `boards.attr` bitmap (currently always 0). Define const flags in code, gate `BoardRepo.List` on user level.
40	- [ ] **[M] Public-key auth alongside password** — `wish.WithPublicKeyAuth` callback that looks up a `user_keys` table. Useful for power users who don't want to type a password every time.
41	- [ ] **[M] Friends / blocklist tables** — needed before pager-settings can be friends-only and before mailbox can have a blocklist. Tables: `user_friends(user_id, friend_id)`, `user_blocks(user_id, blocked_id)`.
42	- [ ] **[M] Structured logging via `log/slog`** — replace the package-level `log` calls in `internal/server` with slog, add per-session correlation IDs.
43	- [ ] **[M] Rate limiting on register / post** — token-bucket per IP for register, per-user for post. The PTT bot wave we'd inevitably attract.
44	- [ ] **[M] SQLite FTS5 search across articles** — virtual table + trigger to mirror inserts. Useful even for a small archive; can be opt-in via build tag if `modernc.org/sqlite` doesn't bundle FTS5.
45	
46	## P3
47	
48	Someday / nice-to-have.
49	
50	- [ ] **[L] Migrate query layer to `sqlc`** — once the schema stops churning. Buys type-safe queries and removes the by-hand `scanX` helpers in repos.
51	- [ ] **[L] Web read-only mirror** — read-only HTTP frontend so non-SSH users can browse / link to articles. Out of scope until the SSH side feels feature-complete.
52	- [ ] **[M] Self-service account deletion + GDPR shape** — out of scope until regulatory pressure arrives. Delete-or-anonymize: pick the path before implementing. Article and push denormalize `author_userid` so we can null user_id while keeping the public string.
53	- [ ] **[M] Prometheus metrics endpoint** — `/metrics` HTTP listener on a separate port, exposing per-board / per-user counters from the broker + DB. Premature without an actual deployment to monitor.
54	- [ ] **[S] GitHub Actions CI: test-race + vet + staticcheck on PR** — gates M2 entry per `docs/operations/00_overview.md`. ~40-line workflow under `.github/workflows/`.
55	- [ ] **[M] Migrate config from CLI flags to env vars (M2 prep)** — additive: env vars override flags. Touches `cmd/sshbbs/main.go:22-26`. See `docs/operations/01_environments.md`.
56	- [ ] **[M] Switch migration runner to `pressly/goose`** — gated on M2 entry (reversibility need). Keep the `fs.FS` / `go:embed` interface. → [research](backlog/goose-migration-switch.md)
57	
58	## P?
59	
60	Needs a spike before committing to a real priority. Tag as `[?/Effort]`.
61	
62	- [ ] **[?/L] Article import from real PTT `.DIR` dumps** — needs spike on `Ptt-official-app/go-bbs` library quality + Big5↔UTF-8 transcoding fidelity before committing. → [research](backlog/ptt-import-spike.md)
63	- [ ] **[?/L] External chat broker (Redis pub/sub) for zero-downtime** — decouples `chat.Broker` from process memory so live presence + in-flight messages survive restart. Spike on whether `SO_REUSEPORT` socket-handoff is cheaper. → [research](backlog/external-chat-broker.md)
64	- [ ] **[?/XL] Postgres migration plan** — only if M4 ever triggers (single-box CPU or write throughput sustained at 70%). Captures the SQLite-WAL → Postgres delta + the two-writer constraint that forces it. → [research](backlog/postgres-migration-plan.md)
65	- [ ] **[?/M] Push round-trip on markdown import** — `markdown.Format` already emits the `<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->` block and `markdown.Parse` reads it back, but `cmd_import.go` and `screen_post_compose` Ctrl+I both drop pushes silently. Restoring them needs (a) per-push author lookup against `users` and a fallback for unknown authors, (b) a permission story so non-admins can't fabricate pushes signed by other users. Probably admin-CLI-only; spike the security model first.
66	- [ ] **[?/S] OSC 52 chunking for >32KB exports** — `screen_article_export` toasts a warning when payload exceeds `osc52Limit` (32KB) but still emits the full sequence; xterm and friends silently truncate. Either chunk via the `52;c;<part>;<id>` extension or fall back automatically to the file-write path.
67	- [ ] **[?/S] Per-user export quota for `data/exports/`** — currently unbounded; one user can fill the disk. Cheapest fix: cap per-user to N files, evict oldest. Implement when disk pressure becomes real.
68	- [ ] **[?/S] Raw-ANSI banner editing via TUI form** — `screen_board_banner_edit` uses `bubbles/textarea`, which filters raw ESC bytes (0x1B) on `SetValue` and on user input. Mods can author plain ASCII art today; raw ANSI banners must currently be written via direct DB / `sshbbs import`-style tooling (none yet). Two paths to evaluate: (a) escape-literal round-trip (`\x1b[31m` ↔ ESC byte on submit/load), or (b) replace textarea with raw-bytes input. Spike on (a) first — non-invasive.

91	- ✅ [2026-04-29] [P1/S] CJK-safe runewidth helpers — `internal/tui/runewidth.go` PadRight/PadLeft/Truncate/Width used in every list rendering; UTF-8 throughout, no Big5.

100	- ✅ [2026-04-29] [P1/S] PTT-style extra hotkeys — `[`/`]` cursor aliases on board list / board view / online list / mail inbox; `[`/`]` sibling navigation on article view via new `ArticleRepo.NeighboursOf`; `Q` quit-to-menu on every list screen. `r` deliberately not bound on board view (PTT reserves it for article view's reply, deferred). Helpers `bodyLineCount`/`viewportLines` keep `G` in sync with the View's render maths. Scroll-key table test covers all three cases (short body, long body, edge).
102	- ✅ [2026-04-29] [P1/S] Online-user list screen — new `screen_online.go` reading `chat.Broker.OnlineList()` (sorted by user_id for stable order); main-menu slot 3 ("線上使用者 Online"), Quit bumped to slot 4. Enter/`l` opens water-balloon compose pre-filled with the cursored user. Test fixture registers fake sessions on a real broker.
103	- ✅ [2026-04-29] [P1/M] Live broadcast of new articles — `ArticleAddedMsg{BoardID, ArticleID, ...}` mirrors `PushAddedMsg`. `screen_post_compose.submit` calls `broker.SendToAll(authorUID, ...)`; `screen_board_view` filters by `BoardID == m.board.ID` and re-fetches the article list from DB so ordering is canonical. Tests assert recording-broker receipt for non-author, exclusion of author, and DB persistence; receiver-side tests cover both same-board and cross-board cases.
104	- ✅ [2026-04-29] [P1/M] Mailbox 站內信 — persistent threaded private messages distinct from water balloons. New migration `0003_add_mail.sql` with thread_id (back-filled to self for roots) + parent_id; `MailRepo` (Insert/ListInboxFor/ListThread/MarkRead/MarkAllReadFor/CountUnreadFor/GetByID) under `writeMu`. Three TUI screens (`screen_mail_inbox` / `_thread` / `_compose`) wired through `NavigateMsg{MailID, MailThreadID}`. Main menu slot 4 ("信箱 Mail"). 50× concurrent-insert race test guards the writeMu discipline.
105	- ✅ [2026-04-30] [P1/L] Welcome 種子文 + 文章 markdown round-trip + 編輯/匯出/匯入 — `internal/markdown/` 純函式套件（hand-rolled YAML-ish frontmatter parser，零新依賴）；`internal/seed/` 把 `articles/welcome.md` 用 `go:embed` 包進 binary，第一次發現 Welcome 看板空就 seed，admin 編輯後重啟不會被覆蓋（看板有任何文就跳過）。新 migration `0005_articles_updated_at.sql` + `ArticleRepo.Update` 鏡像 Delete 的「作者 OR mod+」權限；新 `ScreenArticleEdit` 表單（不綁 h/l）+ `ScreenArticleExport` 唯讀畫面（`1` 純內文 / `2` 含留言 / `3` 寫到 `data/exports/<userid>/` / `c` OSC52 推剪貼簿）。`screen_article_view` 加 `E`（編輯，作者或 mod+）與 `y`（匯出）鍵，handle `ArticleUpdatedMsg` 用 `PushAddedMsg` 樣板 filter+refetch。`screen_post_compose` 加 `Ctrl+I` 從貼上的 markdown 抽 frontmatter 自動填 title/body。新 CLI `sshbbs import --file FILE --board NAME` 走同一個 `markdown.Parse`。煙測通過：seed → 改 → 重啟 → 改的版本保留。
106	- ✅ [2026-05-06] [P2/L] Per-board ANSI/ASCII-art banner — new `boards.banner TEXT` (migration 0006), idempotent seed from `internal/seed/banners/<Name>.txt` with `default.txt` fallback (rule: `banner IS NULL`, mirrors welcome-article seed). Inline render above article list (capped at `maxInlineBannerLines` = 8) using new ANSI-aware `stripCSI`/`bannerVisualWidth`/`bannerTruncate` helpers (existing `Width`/`Truncate`/`PadRight`/`PadLeft` left untouched — table path stays naive). Two new screens: `ScreenBoardSplash` (any-key returns; reached by `b` from board view) and `ScreenBoardBannerEdit` (mod+ only, single-textarea form, no h/l binding, ctrl+s saves via new `BoardRepo.UpdateBanner` permission-gated to `RoleMod`+). Help-line `· b banner / · B edit` shown conditionally. Limitation captured in P? above: `bubbles/textarea` strips raw ESC bytes, so the form is currently plain-ASCII-only — colored banners need to be set via direct DB write until that's resolved.
107	- ✅ [2026-04-30] [P1/L] User permission system — admin / mod / user / guest. New migration `0004_add_user_roles.sql` (role enum + must_change_password). `store.Role` type with rank-ordered `AtLeast`. Reserved usernames extended to `admin` and `guest`; `auth.SeedSystemAccounts` bootstraps both via `-admin-password` flag, default `admin`/`admin` with first-login change forced. `MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked` refuses bootstrapped admin from non-loopback addresses. New `screen_password_change` (form-style) and `screen_admin_users` (paginated list with last-admin guard via `CountByRole`). Article + push delete with owner-or-mod permission check; push cursor (`p`/`P`) targets pushes for the unified `D` confirm flow under `writeMu`+`tx` (50× concurrent-delete race test mirrors the create canary). Read-only enforcement: `Root.navigate` rejects compose screens for guests with toast; cosmetic help-line tweaks per role; guests skip broker registration so multi-session collisions don't matter.

```text
1	# Backlog Research
2	
3	Long-form research, design notes, and paused troubleshooting for items listed in
4	[`../TODO.md`](../TODO.md). One file per topic, named with a slug that matches
5	the TODO entry.
6	
7	## Why this exists
8	
9	`TODO.md` is the **index** — short titles grouped by priority section with
10	effort tags. This folder
11	is the **knowledge base** — the actual investigation, options considered,
12	benchmarks, error messages, and decisions that informed the TODO entry. The goal
13	is **resume-friendliness**: when you (or an agent) come back to an item three
14	months later, the doc here lets you pick up in 5 minutes instead of re-running
15	30 minutes of investigation.
16	
17	This folder is excluded from <DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via
18	.chezmoiignore.tmpl> — it is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing
19	config to deploy.
20	
21	## When to add a doc here
22	
23	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` file when **any** of these apply:
24	
25	- The TODO item carries a `P?` tag (it requires evaluation; record what was tried)
26	- You did meaningful troubleshooting but didn't ship a fix (capture the trace
27	  before it evaporates)
28	- Multiple options were considered (record the trade-offs, not just the winner)
29	- An external blocker exists (waiting on upstream release, host availability)
30	- The implementation is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
31	
32	`[S]` items rarely need a doc unless there's surprising context.
33	
34	## When NOT to add a doc here
35	
36	- Item is `[S]` and obvious — just put the file path in the TODO entry
37	- Already covered by an existing doc page (cross-link instead — that's
```

```text
# External chat broker (Redis pub/sub) for zero-downtime

**Status**: P? — needs spike
**Effort**: ?/L
**Related**: `TODO.md`, `docs/operations/03_deployment.md`, `internal/chat/broker.go`

## Context

The current `chat.Broker` (`internal/chat/broker.go:36-39`) is a
process-local `map[int64][]*Session`. On every restart, live presence
resets to empty and any in-flight `tea.Msg` queued for delivery is
lost. Persistent state (water balloons, mail) survives via the
`delivered_live` flag pattern, so users don't lose messages — but
the online-list flickers and the user experience screams "the server
restarted".

This is the second of two architectural ceilings on zero-downtime
deploys (see `docs/operations/03_deployment.md` and `00_overview.md`
ceiling #2). Decoupling the broker from process memory unblocks one
of the M3 escape hatches.

## Investigation

Not started. The spike below would be the first concrete work.

## Options considered

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|
| A. Redis pub/sub + a presence SET per online user | Mature; well-understood; cheap infra; pub/sub model maps cleanly to current `Broker.Send` / `SendToAll` | New infra dependency; Redis becomes a single point of failure unless replicated |
| B. NATS / JetStream | Better delivery guarantees than pub/sub (queue groups, persistence) | Heavier infra; overkill for in-flight one-line messages |
| C. SQLite as the broker (poll for new rows, `LISTEN`-style) | No new dep | Polling latency contradicts the "live broadcast within 100ms" promise; same single-writer problem already documented |
| D. Socket-handoff via `SO_REUSEPORT` only (`03_deployment.md` option a) | No new dep at all; Linux-native | Solves the *deploy* problem but not the *crash* problem — a hard restart still drops the broker |

Likely answer: **A**, paired with the socket-handoff path (option D)
for deploys. They solve different parts: D handles the planned restart
(no session loss because the old binary keeps existing sessions until
they close naturally), A handles the unplanned crash (presence and
in-flight messages survive in Redis).
```
</details></tool-use>

```md
# article_revisions snapshot table for 板規 amendment history (法律增修條文)

**Status**: P3 — deferred, not currently scheduled
**Effort**: L
**Related**: `TODO.md`, `internal/store/articles.go`, `internal/seed/articles/welcome-rules.md`, the 2026-05-06 pin-articles ship entry

## Context

The 2026-05-06 "置頂文章 / 板規" ship gave us multi-pin articles per board (mod-toggleable, seed-configurable). One board's pinned 板規 article serves as its rulebook; admins amend the rules over time by editing the article body. The user surfaced an explicit interest in modeling this as **法律增修條文** — Taiwan's pattern of preserving old wordings of a law and tracking which amendment changed which clause.

We shipped a **light convention** instead of building this:

- The seed `internal/seed/articles/welcome-rules.md` includes a `## 修訂紀錄` section listing `vX.Y (YYYY-MM-DD) — 變更摘要`.
- Admins edit the pinned article via the existing `E` shortcut and add a new entry to that section.
- No schema, no extra UI; relies on `articles.updated_at` for the most-recent timestamp and on `git log` of the seed file for the long-tail history of admin-edited content.

This works for low-traffic 板規 churn but has known gaps:

1. **No diff** — readers can't see what wording changed between v1.0 and v1.1; only the summary the admin chose to write.
2. **No who** — `updated_at` records *when* but not *who* edited (the article author stays the original admin even if a different mod amended).
3. **No audit trail** — a malicious mod can rewrite history by editing the `## 修訂紀錄` section itself; nothing in the DB stops it.

## Investigation

Not started. The convention has only existed since 2026-05-06; no real-world drift between rule versions yet.

## Options considered

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|
| A. New `article_revisions(article_id, version, title, body, edited_by, edited_at)` table; `ArticleRepo.Update` writes a snapshot on every edit of a *pinned* article | Diff-able; auditable; bounded growth (only pinned articles snapshot); reuses existing edit flow | New table; new index; new TUI screen to view history (otherwise data is unreachable); `Update` becomes two writes under `writeMu` |
| B. Same table, but snapshot **all** article edits, not just pinned | Uniform; no `IF pinned THEN snapshot` branch | Storage growth scales with edit traffic; recommendation push-edits would dominate; mostly noise for non-規 articles |
| C. `git`-as-history: serialize each pinned-article edit to an on-disk markdown file under `data/rules-history/<board>/<article>/<rev>.md`; commit each | Zero schema change; uses `markdown.Format` round-trip we already have | Adds a `data/` directory dependency; backups become more complex; `git` from inside the BBS process is a new failure mode |
| D. Stay with the markdown convention, accept the gaps | Zero work; matches user's "看看是不是有什麼規則可以定一下" exploratory framing | The three gaps above remain |

Likely answer if/when this reactivates: **A** with a hard scope of pinned articles only, plus a new mod-only "view history" screen reachable from the article view (e.g., shift+H). Storage cost is bounded because the only frequent edit-target is the rulebook itself, which is small.

## Open questions

- Should mods be able to *delete* old revisions (compliance with right-to-be-forgotten requests) or are revisions immutable once written?
- Does the history view need its own ANSI render or can it reuse the article-view glamour pipeline per-revision?
- If a 板規 is unpinned, do we keep its prior revisions or garbage-collect them? (Probably keep — unpinning is a moderation action and the trail still has audit value.)

## Triggers to reactivate

Promote out of P3 when **any** of these become real:

- An admin/mod actually edits a 板規 in production and someone asks "what did the old version say?"
- A user disputes a moderation decision and the answer hinges on which version of the rule was in force at the violation time.
- The light `## 修訂紀錄` convention drifts (mods stop adding entries, or contradicting entries appear) — that's the signal the convention can't bear the load alone.

Until then, the 修訂紀錄 markdown section in `welcome-rules.md` is the canonical pattern; agents who add new pinned 板規 should mirror it.

```

Create commit

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Ship pinned 板規 articles: multi-pin, mod-toggleable, seeded

PTT-style M-mark / 置底: mods can pin one or more articles to the top of
a board, serving as 板規 (board rules) or important announcements above
the chronological list. Mirrors the banner-edit pattern: schema column
+ RoleMod-gated repo method + seed frontmatter + TUI shortcut + live
broadcast.

- Migration 0007 adds articles.pinned_at; ListByBoard sorts pinned
  first; ArticleRepo.Update preserves pinned_at across amendments so
  edits to a 板規 don't unpin it.
- markdown frontmatter `pinned: true` round-trips, so seed files
  configure default pins. Seed idempotency softened to a per-run
  emptiness snapshot so welcome.md + welcome-rules.md can co-seed a
  fresh Welcome board (a non-empty board still skips entirely).
- New welcome-rules.md ships an example 板規 (禁止謾罵 / 禁止廣告 /
  違規處理 + 修訂紀錄 v1.0) — markdown convention is the lightweight
  amendment-tracking story; heavy article_revisions table deferred to
  TODO P3/L + backlog/board-rules-amendment-history.md.
- TUI: M shortcut on board view (mod+ only, toggle, cursor follows the
  pinned article across the reorder), [M] row marker on pinned articles,
  ArticlePinChangedMsg broadcast via Broker.SendToAll so other live
  sessions reorder in place.
- 13 new tests across store / markdown / seed / TUI, all green under
  -race.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```
